# Copilot issue title instructions

When generating a GitHub issue title for this repository, apply these rules.

## Format

```
<type>: <imperative summary>
```

- Imperative mood: `Add`, `Fix`, `Investigate` — not `Added`, `Fixing`
- No trailing period
- Max 72 characters
- Make the title short, clear, and descriptive.
- Use imperative mood (e.g., "Fix bug in login flow" not "Fixed bug in login flow").
- ⁠Summarize the main problem or feature in 10 words or fewer.
- Avoid generic titles like "Bug" or "Update code".
- ⁠Include the ticket Jira numbers in the title.
- ⁠Do not include dates in the title.


## Types

| Type | Use when |
|------|----------|
| `Bug` | A test fails unexpectedly, a locator is broken, or a hook misbehaves |
| `Feature` | New test coverage, page object, API client, or framework capability requested |
| `Refactor` | Improve structure, readability, or locator stability without new coverage |
| `Chore` | Dependency update, config change, tooling |
| `Docs` | README, `.github/` AI assets, inline comments |

## Examples

```text
Bug: LoginPage logout locator fails on Firefox after Herokuapp update
Feature: Add CheckoutPage page object and positive flow spec
Refactor: Replace brittle CSS locators in UserProfilePage with getByRole
Chore: Update Playwright to latest and reinstall browser binaries
Docs: Add MCP server setup instructions to README
```

If the issue maps to a Jira ticket, include the key at the end: `... [TC-123]`
